channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: WHO chief lands in Congo to address rare Ebola outbreak amid distrust and insecurity
item.description: The head of the World Health Organization arrived in Congo's capital, Kinshasa, to witness efforts against an outbreak of a rare type of Ebola virus.
item.content:encoded:
The head of the World Health Organization arrived in Congo's capital, Kinshasa, to witness efforts against an outbreak of a rare type of Ebola virus.

(Image credit: Samy Ntumba Shambuyi)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Which first lady feared her husband might be having a stroke? The quiz knows
item.description: This week, the pope took aim at AI, a fancy carmaker dipped a toe in the EV market and a first lady made a surprising comment.
item.content:encoded: This week, the pope took aim at AI, a fancy carmaker dipped a toe in the EV market and a first lady made a surprising comment.
item.dc:creator: Holly J. Morris

item.title: Why the U.S. cattle herd is at a 75-year low — and what it means for beef prices
item.description: The overall number of U.S. beef and dairy cattle has shrunk to its lowest level since 1951. Drought, rising operating costs and increased consolidation are among the causes.
item.content:encoded:
The overall number of U.S. beef and dairy cattle has shrunk to its lowest level since 1951. Drought, rising operating costs and increased consolidation are among the causes.

(Image credit: Luke Sharrett for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Joe Hernandez

item.title: Former AG Pam Bondi to testify before Congress over handling of the Epstein files
item.description: Bondi was ousted from her role as attorney general in April. She will testify Friday in a closed-door hearing about the release of the Epstein files.
item.content:encoded:
Bondi was ousted from her role as attorney general in April. She will testify Friday in a closed-door hearing about the release of the Epstein files.

(Image credit: Win McNamee)
item.dc:creator: Ava Berger

item.title: Replacing aging U.S. voting equipment will take years and billions of dollars
item.description: America's voting systems are getting old. But unless Congress makes a massive financial commitment, a new report finds it could take decades before voting machines are widely replaced.
item.content:encoded:
America's voting systems are getting old. But unless Congress makes a massive financial commitment, a new report finds it could take decades before voting machines are widely replaced.

(Image credit: Kathleen Flynn)
item.dc:creator: Miles Parks

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: The U.S. says it's moving closer to a deal to end the Iran war despite continued fighting, DOJ opens investigation into Trump accuser E. Jean Carroll, Bari Weiss wants to reinvent CBS' "60 Minutes."
item.content:encoded: The U.S. says it's moving closer to a deal to end the Iran war despite continued fighting, DOJ opens investigation into Trump accuser E. Jean Carroll, Bari Weiss wants to reinvent CBS' "60 Minutes."
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: Blue Origin rocket explodes on the launch pad during an engine-firing test
item.description: A rocket belonging to Jeff Bezos' Blue Origin exploded during a test at the launch pad Thursday night, ahead of a satellite launch planned for next week.
item.content:encoded:
A rocket belonging to Jeff Bezos' Blue Origin exploded during a test at the launch pad Thursday night, ahead of a satellite launch planned for next week.

(Image credit: John Raoux)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Shrey Parikh, 14, wins the Scripps Spelling Bee after a nail-biting 'spell-off'
item.description: This is the 101st year of the national spelling competition, and the third time it's been decided by a rapid-fire "spell-off" since the practice was introduced in 2021.
item.content:encoded:
This is the 101st year of the national spelling competition, and the third time it's been decided by a rapid-fire "spell-off" since the practice was introduced in 2021.

(Image credit: Allison Robbert)
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman

item.title: Treasury Department prepares $250 bill with Trump's face on it
item.description: Congress needs to pass legislation in order for the bill's printing
item.content:encoded:
Congress needs to pass legislation in order for the bill's printing

(Image credit: KENT NISHIMURA)
item.dc:creator: Deepa Shivaram

item.title: Former senior CIA officer took home gold bars and millions in cash, FBI says
item.description: David Rush, a former senior CIA officer, is accused of theft and making false statements. The trove of gold and cash went missing from his CIA storage space — but it was found in his Virginia home.
item.content:encoded: David Rush, a former senior CIA officer, is accused of theft and making false statements. The trove of gold and cash went missing from his CIA storage space — but it was found in his Virginia home.
item.dc:creator: Bill Chappell
